subject: Computer Networking
title: ARP: Finding Hardware Addresses
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: ARP: Finding Hardware Addresses.

hook.text: Your computer knows the IP address of a neighbor device. But the network card needs a physical address to deliver.

buildup.text: ARP — Address Resolution Protocol — maps IP addresses to MAC addresses on a local network.

discovery.text: It broadcasts a question: 'Who has 192.168.1.5?' The owner replies with its MAC address.

twist.text: ARP has no security. An attacker can reply with a fake MAC address and intercept your traffic.

climax.text: ARP spoofing is one of the oldest network attacks. Modern switches use protections to prevent it.

punchline.text: ARP translates logical addresses to physical ones. Simple but essential.
